title: Restore fully functional Create Client form submission

requirements.summary: Fix ClientDetailPage Create/Save button to properly call createClient mutation and navigate on success
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/clients/new' renders the ClientDetailPage in create mode with all form fields empty.
Filling in the form fields (name, phone, address, notes, email) and clicking the 'Create' / 'Save' button triggers the createClient mutation.
On successful createClient response the user is navigated back to '/clients' and the new client appears in the list.
If createClient fails, an error message is displayed and the user remains on the form.
The button is never rendered in a permanently disabled state when the form has valid input.
No console errors or unhandled promise rejections occur during the create flow.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the form submission handler for create mode. Ensure the Save/Create button has a proper onClick or form onSubmit handler that calls event.preventDefault(), invokes the createClient mutation from useQueries with the correct field values from form state, awaits the result or uses onSuccess callback, and navigates to '/clients' on success using the router navigate function. Verify the button is not permanently disabled when form fields are filled. Add error state display for failed mutations. Ensure the createClient mutation is correctly wired to the backend actor.

requirements.summary: Fix JobDetailPage Create/Save button to properly call createJob mutation and navigate on success
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to '/jobs/new' renders JobDetailPage in create mode with form fields empty.
Clicking 'Create' / 'Save' with valid input calls the createJob mutation.
On success the user is navigated away from the create form.
On failure an error message is shown and the user remains on the form.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the form submission handler for create mode. Ensure the Save/Create button has a proper onClick or form onSubmit handler that calls event.preventDefault(), invokes the createJob mutation from useQueries with the correct field values matching the Job type from backend.d.ts, awaits the result or uses onSuccess callback, and navigates to '/jobs' or the new job detail page on success. Verify the button is not permanently disabled when required fields are filled. Add error state display for failed mutations. Ensure the createJob mutation is correctly wired to the backend actor.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix form submission handlers across ClientDetailPage and JobDetailPage for proper event handling and async flow
acceptanceCriteria: Form submission does not cause a full page reload.
Mutation arguments match the expected backend input types.
Success and error callbacks execute reliably after mutation resolution.
No orphaned async calls that resolve after the component has unmounted.

file_operations.description: Audit all form submission handlers (onSubmit on form elements, onClick on buttons). Ensure event.preventDefault() is called at the start of each handler to prevent page reloads. Verify mutation function calls use the correct argument shapes matching the Client type from backend.d.ts (id as bigint, required fields: name, phone, address, notes; optional fields: email, googleReviewUrl). Ensure async mutation calls are properly awaited or use mutation.mutateAsync with .then/.catch or React Query's onSuccess/onError callbacks so navigation and error handling fire correctly. Add cleanup to prevent state updates after unmount if needed.

Audit all form submission handlers (onSubmit on form elements, onClick on buttons). Ensure event.preventDefault() is called at the start of each handler to prevent page reloads. Verify mutation function calls use the correct argument shapes matching the Job type from backend.d.ts (id as bigint, required fields: clientId, date, tech, status, notes, laborLineItems, photos; optional fields: estimate, waiverType, stripePaymentId). Ensure async mutation calls are properly awaited or use mutation.mutateAsync with .then/.catch or React Query's onSuccess/onError callbacks so navigation and error handling fire correctly. Add cleanup to prevent state updates after unmount if needed.
